perfect: I absolutely loved the Journal mechanic. It's incredible how the game solves the "what's the next step?" question so naturally.
The immense mystery and story created are very enjoyable.
The art design and that unique visual language perfectly complemented the game's melancholic yet intriguing atmosphere.
good: Traveling between planets with our spaceship and getting lost in that vast void was very enjoyable at first.
poor: I really wished there was voice acting in the dialogues; hearing that deep story from the characters' voices would have taken the atmosphere to a whole new level.
It took me some time to get used to the ship and character controls; they can feel clunky at times.
Having to walk through similar discoveries and paths over and over again tested my patience after a certain point.
While solving mysteries is very fun, the pure gameplay mechanics fall into repetition quite easily, and the "game" part lags behind the excitement of discovery.

mechanics.title: Journal
mechanics.text:
* A great system that completely eliminates the "what do I do now?" dilemma in the game.
* Following the question marks and solving that giant mystery piece by piece gave me an incredible sense of satisfaction.
* Even dying and starting everything from the beginning turned from a frustrating experience into a meaningful process thanks to this mechanic.

name: Outer Wilds

perfect: I loved the art design. I felt like I was making pizza inside a hand-drawn fairy tale book.
good: The story flow and interactions with characters really sucked me in.
The sound effects are incredibly satisfying.
The Garden mechanic was a great breathing point for me.
poor: The core gameplay loop started to bore me after a while. At one point, I only kept playing because I was curious about what would happen in the story.
The upgrades felt a bit insufficient to me. They generally only serve to automate the tasks I do and are not very creative.

terrible.text: I struggled a lot with User Experience (UX); it's full of choices that feel way behind today's standards and outdated.
children: For example, once I pick up the pizza cutter, there's no going back; I must finish that process. Being unable to cancel even if I do something wrong really stressed me out.

terrible: The PC version reeks of mobile game behavior everywhere; the in-game purchases (IAP) I encountered at every corner ruined my enjoyment by giving a "free-to-play" feel.

Technically, I experienced a complete fiasco. Due to cloud save problems, hours of my effort were deleted multiple times. The game becoming unplayable this way was the final straw for me, and I had to return it.

mechanics.title: Garden
mechanics.text:
* The garden mechanic brings a fresh breath to the game's routine pace.
* Adding vegetables and fruits I grew myself to the pizzas is very enjoyable.

name: Good Pizza, Great Pizza
